methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $246M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $529M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 24.4x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: DEEPX
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series C), post-money $529M

data.narrative:
FuriosaAI is a Series C semiconductor chip company that has raised $246M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 4 private peers in the same sector at similar or adjacent stages—SambaNova Systems ($5.0B, Series D), Tenstorrent ($2.7B, Series D), Positron AI ($1.0B, Series B), and DEEPX ($529M, Series C)—alongside 3 public semiconductor comps (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) for directional context.

Two triangulation methods proved applicable. Stage-progression analysis, using the $246M total raised as a proxy baseline and applying a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical of Series C advancement, implies a valuation of $418M. Peer-median analysis across the 4 private comparables yields $1.9B, with a range spanning $529M to $5.0B. Public-comp anchoring via price-to-sales (24.4x median across the semiconductor universe) could not be applied due to the absence of disclosed revenue for FuriosaAI.

The two applicable methods triangulate to a range of $355M (low) to $2.1B (high), with a median of $1.1B. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty around whether FuriosaAI's competitive position, product maturity, and market traction align more closely with DEEPX at the lower end or with the Series D benchmarks. Revenue visibility and near-term commercialization trajectories remain key drivers of where within this band a fair valuation settles.
